title: Pediatric wheeze follow-up

red_flags.text: Supports sudden or severe breathing difficulty, cardiac or chest symptoms, cyanosis, inability to speak full sentences, confusion or agitation, stridor or audible wheeze, immobility or trauma, orthopnoea, status asthmaticus, and near-fatal asthma history as clinician-assessed prompts.
red_flags.rationale: Supports respiratory warning features for paediatric wheeze follow-up; no decision is generated.
red_flags.evidence_extract: Supports sudden or severe breathing difficulty, cardiac or chest symptoms, cyanosis, inability to speak full sentences, confusion or agitation, stridor or audible wheeze, immobility or trauma, orthopnoea, status asthmaticus, and near-fatal asthma history as clinician-assessed prompts.

source.title: DHA Telehealth Clinical Guidelines for Virtual Management of Asthma – 38

examination.text: Supports wheeze, cough, breathlessness, chest tightness, variation, nocturnal or seasonal pattern, triggers, exercise and occupational context, atopy or family history, bronchodilator response, respiratory examination, peak flow, and objective confirmation.
examination.rationale: Supports asthma symptom and clinician-diagnosis context for paediatric wheeze follow-up; no decision is generated.
examination.evidence_extract: Supports wheeze, cough, breathlessness, chest tightness, variation, nocturnal or seasonal pattern, triggers, exercise and occupational context, atopy or family history, bronchodilator response, respiratory examination, peak flow, and objective confirmation.

source.title: DHA Telehealth Clinical Guidelines for Virtual Management of Asthma – 38

referral.text: Supports review of alternative or comorbid causes, adherence, inhaler technique, smoking, occupational, psychosocial and environmental factors, treatment response, device review, and clinician-led referral.
referral.rationale: Supports uncontrolled-asthma review and clinician-led referral context for paediatric wheeze follow-up; no decision is generated.
referral.evidence_extract: Supports review of alternative or comorbid causes, adherence, inhaler technique, smoking, occupational, psychosocial and environmental factors, treatment response, device review, and clinician-led referral.

source.title: DHA Telehealth Clinical Guidelines for Virtual Management of Asthma – 38

source_limitations: All item-level mappings remain unsupported pending separate clinician review and signed approval.
Age, wheeze onset and pattern, triggers, cough, breathing, night symptoms, reliever use, adherence, examination, diagnosis and clinician plan remain absent.
No diagnosis, interpretation, investigation, treatment, medicine, dose, procedure, referral, escalation or disposition is generated for paediatric wheeze follow-up.
Any legacy item not explicitly mapped to an exact section remains unsupported pending qualified clinician review.
No mapping authorises an automatic diagnosis, differential, investigation, management decision, medicine, dose, procedure, referral, urgency conclusion, discharge decision, or patient instruction.
curation_policy: Direct guideline curation: retain only explicitly mapped content; rewrite only with explicit item mapping; remove unmapped legacy content; add only committed exact-section evidence summaries.
